title: Acer niveum
taxonomy: Acer niveum is classified within the family Sapindaceae, but it is more commonly associated with the family Sapindaceae being part of a broader grouping known as the Sapindales order. The genus Acer, however, is actually associated with the family Sapindaceae being part of a broader grouping known as the Sapindales order and also the Sapindales order being part of the larger group known as the Sapindales clade, which is part of the larger grouping known as the rosids clade in the APG III system. The APG III system is the Angiosperm Phylogeny Group III system. The Sapindales order is also part of the larger group known as the rosid clade, which is part of the larger grouping known as the core eudicots in the APG III system.

preparations: Acer niveum, also known as the Snow Maple, has limited information available on its herbal preparations. However, some sources suggest that the plant's bark and leaves have been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. The bark is believed to have anti-inflammatory properties, and the leaves may possess antioxidant and antimicrobial properties.
